column2: The D+2 check-in SMS after the discovery call is the single most conversion-critical message. In every thread reviewed, a purchase followed when Lauren sent a morning check-in asking if the lead had questions about the invoice. The bot MUST send this proactively, personalized to the call discussion.

NOT stored in GHL. The 88+ contacts tagged 'smartbot | import' were imported FROM a prior AI tool — those chat transcripts exist only in the source SmartBot platform. This spreadsheet contains all available GHL SMS data.

+18337154447 = Primary LLMD number (Lauren + all automated workflows). +14352925563 = Janice setter number only. CloseBot should be registered to +18337154447 to maintain continuity with leads who already have Lauren's number.

Janice (userId: 1N3n2voecT7o7g5rqyH2), Erin (userId: lZnQHFibOdnLt3KbGw5l), Lauren (userId: Ptq5d0pW1GYT2uFJraIM). All three used slight variations of the same base template. Erin's variant referenced Healthie import.

Automated no-show SMS containing 'You deserve to feel clear, energized, and balanced again' was blocked by carrier (Error 30007 on Anastasia Taylor and Darion Hansen threads). Lauren's manual version was always delivered. Avoid wellness-claim language in automated SMS.
Most 'not interested' responses are single-sentence polite declines or STOP unsubscribes. Very few are argumentative. Bot exit should be short and warm — no re-pitch.
Leads who say 'what time?' or go slow are experiencing scheduling friction, not disinterest. Offering specific time slots converts these. Hard STOP = genuine disinterest. Treat differently.

Most common questions after the call: (1) How do I buy the 3-month protocol? (2) Can you send the COA? (3) What state are you in? (4) Can you update the invoice for a second person? (5) Where is my shipment? Bot should proactively offer help with next steps rather than re-pitching.
If a contact already has cjc+ipamorelin, nad+, tirzepatide, or any other purchase tag, route to 'Already a Patient' scenario — NOT the setter flow.

Mode 1 — Setter (pre-call): Warm, no-pressure, curiosity-led. Mirror Janice's voice. Goal: book 15-min discovery call.
Mode 2 — Post-call nurture: Mirror Lauren's follow-up voice. Goal: remove purchase friction, check for questions, reference call discussion.
